Manx: Strateysh son y Ghaelg
Notes: Title page; not translated in the original

Manx: Coontey yn Chied Vlein 2022-2032
Notes: Title page; not translated in the original

Manx: Jeant aarloo liorish Jeebin - Manx Language Network
Notes: Title page; not translated in the original

Manx: Goan-Foslee

English: The second joint Manx Language Strategy (2022 – 2032) is a ten-year vision for effective, coordinated support for the promotion and delivery of the Manx language.
Manx: She ashlish jeih bleeaney ta’n nah Strateysh copharteeagh son y Ghaelg (2022 - 2032) dy chur cooney fondagh as er ny cho-oardaghey cour cur er e toshiaght as cur ayns bree yn ghlare Vanninagh.

English: This interim report relates to the first year of the strategy.
Manx: Ta’n tuarastyl shallidagh shoh bentyn rish y chied vlein jeh’n strateysh.

English: Jeebin, the Manx Language Network, is made up of language professionals who have worked together with the community to devise the Manx Language Strategy, deliver the action plans, and to produce this report.
Manx: Ta Jeebin (Manx Language Network), jeant seose jeh sleih profeshoonagh ta gobbragh lesh y ghlare, t’er cho-obbragh marish y phobble dy chroo yn Strateysh son y Ghaelg, dy chur ayns bree ny plannyn-jantys, as dy chummey yn tuarastyl shoh.

English: Jeebin acts as a coordinating and facilitating network, and individual members also represent many of the voluntary language bodies who support the Manx language in a variety of ways.
Manx: Ta Jeebin gaghtey myr moggyl ta co-oardaghey as fosley yn raad, as ta olteynyn er-lheh shassoo neesht son ymmodee jeh ny sheshaghtyn-glare arryltagh ta cummal seose y Ghaelg er caghlaaghyn aght.

English: We are passionate advocates and supporters of Manx, and we share the aspiration of the Manx language community that our language continues to grow in the hearts of more and more people.
Manx: Ta shin moylley as cummal seose y Ghaelg er aght jeean as ta’n un yeearree ain as t’ec yn phobble Gaelgagh, ta shen dy ghra dy jean nyn nglare tannaghtyn dy aase ayns creeaghyn ny shlee as ny shlee dy ‘leih.

English: The focus of the very first joint Manx Language Strategy (2017 – 2022) was to work together effectively, coordinating activity to avoid duplication, and to focus on the strategic support needed to develop the use of Manx.
Manx: Ayns yn chied Strateysh copharteeagh hene son y Ghaelg (2017 - 2022) va tastey er-lheh currit da ny reddyn shoh: gobbragh cooidjagh er aght fondagh, co-oardaghey obbyr dy haghney dooblaghey nyn eabyn, as cur tooilley geill da’n chooney strateyshagh ta ymmyrçhagh dy vishaghey ymmyd y Ghaelg.

English: Our focus for the 2022 – 2032 strategy is again on awareness and education, and on working together to make the Manx language accessible to everyone, everywhere.
Manx: Ayns y strateysh cour ny bleeantyn 2022 - 2032 ta shin cur tastey er-lheh reesht da ynsagh as doostey sym er y ghlare, da gobbragh cooidjagh dy ‘osley magh yn Ghaelg da dy chooilley pheiagh, dy chooilley voayl.

English: It sees an increased focus on training and advocacy, the development of new resources, and the exploration of ambitions for expanded education provision.
Manx: Ta’n strateysh shoh cur tooilley geill da traenal as shirrey foayr y theay, da croo couyryn noa, as da smooinaghtyn er ny deanyn s’cooie bentyn da kiarail ynsagh shlea.

English: We want to grow the number of speakers and learners of Manx, on-island and worldwide, and to support the community in using Manx on a daily basis.
Manx: B’laik lhien yn earroo dy loayrtee as ynseydee ny Gaelgey y vishaghey, ayns Mannin as feiy ny cruinney, as cooney lesh y phobble dy yannoo ymmyd jeh’n Ghaelg gagh laa.

English: Since the publication of the 2022 – 2032 Manx Language Strategy, Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh (Manx Language Research Group) has become a member of Jeebin, representing the position of Manx within academia.
Manx: Neayr’s cur magh y Strateysh son y Ghaelg cour 2022 - 2032, ta Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh er jeet dy ve ny oltey jeh Jeebin, shassoo son y Ghaelg ayns cooishyn ard-schoillaragh.

English: Academic research plays a pivotal role in advancing language development by shedding light on the unique linguistic and cultural nuances of the Manx language, ultimately helping to preserve and promote our rich cultural heritage.
Manx: Ta ronsaghey ard-schoillaragh cloie ayrn feer scanshoil ayns cur er e toshiaght lhiasaghey’n ghlare liorish cur gys y toilshey mynphoyntyn unnaneagh glareydagh as cultooroil y Ghaelg, ta cooney lesh freayll as cur er e toshiaght nyn eiraght chultooroil verçhagh.

English: It also contributes towards practical resource development.
Manx: T’eh cur cooney da lhiasaghey couyryn er aght practicoil neesht.

English: In addition, research on language policy can inform the development of public policy for the language.
Manx: Chammah’s shoh, foddee ronsaghey er polasee glare cur fys share dooin mychione lhiasaghey polasee theayagh son y ghlare.

English: The Isle of Man has always benefitted from worldwide support from minority and minoritised language communities, and we report regularly to the British Irish Council (BIC) work-stream for Indigenous, Minority and Lesser used languages (IML), learn from their expertise and benefit from their generosity in sharing ideas and resources.
Manx: Ta Mannin er gheddyn vondeish er dy rieau jeh cooney feiy ny cruinney veih pobbleyn myn-ghlaraghyn, as pobbleyn glaraghyn tranlaasit, as ta shin cur coontey dy reiltagh da stroo-obbyr yn Choonceil Ghoaldagh-Yernagh (CGY) son Glaraghyn Dooghyssagh, Myn-Ghlaraghyn as Glaraghyn ta Ymmyd Sloo jeant jeu (DMYS), ta shin gynsagh veih’n oalaght oc as geddyn vondeish jeh’n aght t’ad rheynn smooinaghtyn as couyryn marin dy feoilt.

English: We are also Working to support Part III of the European Charter for Regional or Minority Languages, which challenges us to make Manx relevant to all aspects of society and life and report to the Council of Europe on progress.
Manx: Ta shin gobbragh neesht dy chummal seose Ayrn III jeh Conaant Coonceil yn Oarpey son Glaraghyn Ardjynagh ny Myn-Ghlaraghyn. Ta shoh doolaney shin dy yannoo yn Ghaelg cooie da dy chooilley ayrn jeh sheshaght as bea, as dy chur coontey da Coonceil yn Oarpey mychione yn aght ta shin goll er nyn doshiaght.

English: Learning and using Manx not only has a positive impact on personal well-being, but also provides economic benefits, helps to positively promote the Isle of Man internationally and strengthens our cultural and national identity.
Manx: Cha nel gynsagh as jannoo ymmyd jeh’n Ghaelg ny lomarcan jannoo ny share jeh’n vea phersoonagh ain, agh chammah’s shen t’eh cur dooin neesht vondeishyn tarmaynagh, t’eh cooney lhien dy chur Mannin er e toshiaght ‘sy theihll eddyr-ashoonagh as t’eh niartaghey yn enney cultooroil as ashoonagh ain.

English: We remain passionate about working together to take the development of Manx forward in ways that are inclusive and accessible to all.
Manx: Ta shin tannaghtyn dy ve feer aggindagh dy obbragh cooidjagh nish as ‘sy traa ry-heet dy chur y Ghaelg er e toshiaght er aghtyn ta goaill stiagh as lhiggey stiagh dy chooilley pheiagh.

English: Theme 1: Appropriate & accessible resources
Manx: Cooish 1: Couyryn cooie ta ry-gheddyn dy haashagh.

English: Strategic Commitment
Manx: Dean strateyshagh

English: • We will encourage the development, publication and promotion of new resources.
Manx: Nee mayd greinnaghey croo, cur-magh as cur er nyn doshiaght jeh couyryn noa.

English: • We will ensure any developed resources are coordinated, appropriate and of good quality, across all ages and stages.
Manx: Nee mayd shickyraghey dy bee couyryn erbee vees crooit er nyn go-oardaghey, cooie as jeh foaynoo mie, bentyn da dy chooilley eash as keim.

English: • We will work to ensure greater digitisation of Manx Language resources at all levels.
Manx: Nee mayd gobbragh dy hickyraghey dy bee tooilley couyryn Gaelgagh currit ayns cummey bun-earrooagh ec dy chooilley cheim.

English: Progression
Manx: Goll er e hoshiaght

English: Mooinjer Veggey has been instrumental in creating several new resources for primary school, preschool, and parents.
Manx: Ta Mooinjer Veggey er ve gastey as breeoil ayns croo shiartanse dy chouyryn noa cour bunscoillyn, roie-scoillyn, as paarantyn.

English: These resources include two Viking stories authored by Annie Kissack.
Manx: Mastey ny couyryn shoh ta daa skeeal Wiggynagh scruit liorish Annie Kissack.

English: Furthermore, they have published Adrian Cain’s translation of Tom Palmer’s After the War targeted at KS2 readers at Bunscoill Ghaelgagh.
Manx: Ny sodjey na shoh, t’ad er chur magh çhyndaays Adrian Cain jeh ‘After the War’ liorish Tom Palmer cour lhaihderyn ec y Vunscoill Ghaelgagh.

English: Both texts were printed with the support of Culture Vannin.
Manx: Va’n daa heks prentit lesh cooney veih Culture Vannin.

English: The four levels of the Loayr Gaelg’ (Speak Manx) adult-learners course from Culture Vannin are now complete.
Manx: Ta ny kiare keimyn jeh coorse Culture Vannin son ynseydee aasit ‘Loayr Gaelg’ nish dy slane cooilleenit.

English: Resources are available online on the Learn Manx website, in the form of PDF course books accompanied by nearly 200 supporting revision sound files.
Manx: Ta couyryn ry-gheddyn er-linney er yn ynnyd-eggey Learn Manx, ayns cummey lioaryn coorse PDF as ta bunnys 200 coadan sheean kiarit maroo cour aastudeyrys.

English: Culture Vannin have also printed copies used by in-person Manx classes.
Manx: Ta Culture Vannin er phrental copy-yn neesht ta ymmyd jeant jeu ayns brastyllyn Gaelgagh eddin ry eddin.

English: Culture Vannin have been pleased to supply other Manx organisations with copies to help facilitate community adult Manx lessons.
Manx: Ta Culture Vannin er ve jeant booiagh dy chiarail copy-yn da sheshaghtyn Gaelgagh elley dy ‘osley yn raad da brastyllyn Gaelgagh cour sleih aasit mastey’n phobble.

English: Bunscoill Ghaelgagh has developed an in-house phonics scheme and staff training to support the teaching of systematic synthetic phonics through Manx from Reception to Year 2 with continuity to English phonics schemes in KS2.
Manx: Ta’n Vunscoill Ghaelgagh er chroo skeim fonnickyn daue hene as traenal son y skimmee dy niartaghey ynsagh fonnickyn synthetick systematickagh ayns Gaelg veih Blein Toshee gys Blein 2 ta co-chiangley rish skeimyn fonnickyn Baarlagh ayns Ard-Cheim 2.

English: The Advisory Teacher for the Manx Curriculum and Bunscoill Ghaelgagh have jointly published a bilingual playground games pack, which is available to all schools, nurseries, and toddler groups.
Manx: Ta’n Ynseyder-Coyrlee son y Churriglym Manninagh as yn Vunscoill Ghaelgagh cooidjagh er chur magh kionnan gammanyn close-scoill daa-hengagh, ta ry-gheddyn da ooilley ny scoillyn, roie-scoillyn as possanyn-cloie.

English: Yn Skimmee Gaelgagh (the Manx Language Service) has created four learners' videos and content for young learners, some of which are now available online.
Manx: Ta’n Skimmee Gaelgagh er chroo kiare feeshanyn cour sleih ta gynsagh as tooilley reddyn cour ynseydee aegey, paart jeu ta ry-gheddyn nish er-linney.

English: They are also working on developing guided learning activities and interactive games to complement these videos.
Manx: T’ad gobbragh er croo roortyssyn ynsagh leeidit as gammanyn eddyr-obbragh dy gholl marish ny feeshanyn shoh.

English: Additionally, they have overseen the publication of translations of Beatrix Potter’s The Tale of Jemima Puddleduck and The Tale of Tom Kitten by young speakers and have contributed digitised archive material to the Manx Corpus Search website.
Manx: Ny sodjey, t’ad er n’oaseiraghey cur magh çhyndaayssyn jeant liorish loayrtee aegey jeh The Tale of Jemima Puddleduck as The Tale of Tom Kitten liorish Beatrix Potter. Chammah’s shoh t’ad er chur tooilley cooid ass y tashtey-recortys ayns cummey bun-earrooagh er ynnyd-eggey y Chorpus Gaelgagh.

English: Dr Christopher Lewin is actively working on a new edition of Edmund Goodwin's First Lessons in Manx for Yn Cheshaght Ghailckagh (Manx Language Society).
Manx: Ta Dr. Christopher Lewin gobbragh dy breeoil er cur magh noa jeh First Lessons in Manx liorish Edmund Goodwin son Yn Çheshaght Ghailckagh.

English: Additionally, Professor Max W. Wheeler's new edition of Pargeiys Caillit (Paradise Lost) is nearing publication, also to be released by Yn Cheshaght Ghailckagh.
Manx: Chammah’s shen ta cur magh noa yn Olloo Max W. Wheeler jeh Pargeiys Caillit bunnys aarloo dy ve prentit, vees currit magh liorish Yn Çheshaght Ghailckagh neesht.

English: The Manx Corpus website has grown significantly and now contains 1,826,332 words spanning 719 documents.
Manx: Ta ynnyd-eggey yn Chorpus Gaelgagh er n’aase dy jarroo as nish t’eh cummal 1,826,332 dy ‘ocklyn voish 719 dy ghocamadyn.

English: This includes a substantial portion of printed material from the eighteenth century, such as the Bible, as well as numerous shorter items, both printed and manuscript, from the nineteenth century.
Manx: Ta shoh goaill stiagh cooid vooar dy stoo prentit veih’n hoghtoo cheead jeig, myr yn Vible, as earroo mooar dy heksyn s’girrey, chammah prentit as ayns cummey laue-screeuyn, veih’n nuyoo cheead jeig.

English: A selection of material from the twentieth century to the present, primarily from magazines and newspapers, has also been incorporated.
Manx: Ta reih dy heksyn veih’n ‘eedoo cheead gys y laa t’ayn nish, earishlioaryn as pabyryn-naight son y chooid smoo, er ve currit stiagh neesht.

English: Furthermore, a pilot sample of timestamped audio-visual material, including interviews and native speaker recordings, has been added to the corpus.
Manx: Ny sodjey, ta sambyl prowallagh jeh cooid eaishtagh-jeeaghyn lesh cowraghyn-traa, goaill stiagh quaaltyssyn as recortyssyn jeh loayrtee dooghyssagh er ve currit stiagh ayns y Chorpus.

English: Most of these materials are bilingual, featuring either the original English text or a new translation, and are fully searchable in both languages.
Manx: Ta’n chooid smoo jeh’n stoo shoh daa-hengagh, eddyr lesh yn teks bunneydagh Baarlagh ny lesh çhyndaays noa, as foddee sleih ronsagh ayns y daa ghlare.

English: As part of the Aberystwyth University Manx sermon project which several Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh members have been involved with, 90 eighteenth century manuscript sermons have been digitally transcribed and will be added to the corpus in due course.
Manx: Myr ayrn jeh shalee sharmaneyn Gaelgagh Ollooscoill Aberystwyth, aynjee ghow shiartanse dy olteynyn y Phossan Ronsee Gaelgagh ayrn, hie kiare-feed as jeih sharmaneyn ayns cummey laue-screeuyn veih’n nuyoo cheead jeig er mac-screeu er aght bun-earrooagh as bee ad er nyn goyrt gys y Chorpus roish foddey.

English: This project was funded by the British Academy, Leverhulme Trust and Culture Vannin.
Manx: Va argid kiarit cour y çhalee shoh liorish yn Academee Goaldagh, Treisht Leverhulme as Culture Vannin.

English: They are exploring potential funding sources which would allow some of the work on the Corpus to be carried out.
Manx: T’ad ronsaghey farraneyn argidoil possibyl yinnagh lhiggey da paart jeh’n obbyr er y Chorpus ve cooilleenit.

English: Culture Vannin has developed new resources, including an interactive place names map designed to assist with pronunciation.
Manx: Ta Culture Vannin er chroo couyryn noa, ny mast’oc ta map eddyr-obbragh dy enmyn buill ta kiarit dy chooney lesh fockley magh.

English: This includes an alphabetised section featuring hundreds of house names in Manx, aiding the community in understanding and using these names with English-Manx and Manx-English translations, along with sound files.
Manx: Ta shoh goaill stiagh rheynn abbyrlhitagh lhome-lane dy enmyn thie ayns Gaelg, vees cooney lesh y phobble dy hoiggal as jannoo ymmyd jeh ny henmyn shoh, ta çhyndaayssyn Baarle-Gaelg as Gaelg-Baarle ayn chammah’s coadanyn sheean.

English: The Spoken Dictionary section has been expanded with a substantial number of additional sound files.
Manx: Ta’n Fockleyr Loayrit er ve jeant ny smoo as earroo feeu baght dy choadanyn sheean noa er nyn goyrt stiagh.

English: Storfys, a database of Manx language resources, is now available in digital format, and is accessible to all Jeebin members through Microsoft Teams.
Manx: Ta ‘Stoyr-fys’, stoyr-fysseree dy chouyryn Gaelgagh ry-gheddyn nish ayns cummey bun-earrooagh, as t’eh foshlit da dy chooilley oltey jeh Jeebin trooid Microsoft Teams.

English: Manx National Heritage continues to provide resources to help teach Manx history through Manx by translating more school loan boxes into Manx for use by the Skimmee Gaelgagh and the Bunscoill Ghaelgagh.
Manx: Ta Eiraght Ashoonagh Vannin tannaghtyn dy chiarail couyryn dy chooney lesh gynsagh shennaghys Manninagh ayns Gaelg liorish çhyndaa tooilley kishtaghyn-eeasee gys Gaelg son ymmyd yn Skimmee Gaelgagh as yn Vunscoill Ghaelgagh.

English: Future Work
Manx: Obbyr ‘sy Traa Ry-Heet

English: Bunscoill Ghaelgagh will be developing and trialling a year-long beginner’s course with digital resources aimed at Bunscoill Ghaelgagh families, but available online for any beginner learners.
Manx: Bee’n Vunscoill Ghaelgagh lhiasaghey as prowal coorse toshiaghteyryn blein er-lhiurid cour lughtyn-thie cloan yn Vunscoill Ghaelgagh, agh ry-gheddyn er-linney da ynseydee erbee ta goaill toshiaght.

English: Bunscoill Ghaelgagh will continue training pupils in media skills as part of their creative curriculum and to produce entertaining, contemporary content for all Manx learners and speakers.
Manx: Nee’n Vunscoill Ghaelgagh tannaghtyn dy chur traenal da paitçhyn y scoill mychione schleiyn meanyn myr ayrn jeh nyn gurriglym crootagh as dy yientyn cooid cho-emshyragh, taitnyssagh cour dy chooilley pheiagh ta gynsagh Gaelg as cour Gaelgeyryn.

English: Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh has several significant goals for the development of the corpus:
Manx: Ta shiartanse dy gheanyn cowreydagh ec Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh bentyn da lhiasaghey yn Chorpus:

English: The foremost priority is to finalise the addition of all eighteenth and early nineteenth- century printed religious materials to the Corpus.
Manx: Yn ard-dean t’eh dy chooilleeney cur stiagh er y Chorpus ooilley yn stoo crauee prentit ayns yn hoghtoo cheead jeig as toshiaght yn nuyoo cheead jeig.

English: Furthermore, members plan to incorporate a substantial volume of eighteenth-century manuscript material.
Manx: Ny sodjey, ta olteynyn plannal dy ghoaill stiagh towse mooar dy stoo ayns cummey laue-screeuyn veih’n hoghtoo cheead jeig.

English: The focus will initially be on sermons, followed by carvals and other text types from the same era.
Manx: Da sharmaneyn vees tastey er-lheh currit hoshiaght, as lurg shen da carvalyn as teksyn elley veih’n un lhing.

English: They will begin the process of adding manuscript images linked to the texts, enhancing the visual aspect of the Corpus.
Manx: Gowee ad toshiaght er yn obbyr jeh cur stiagh caslyssyn laue-screeuyn ta kianglt rish ny teksyn, vees jannoo ny share tuarystal yn Chorpus.

English: Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh is committed to completing the addition of all available native speaker recordings, complemented by bilingual transcripts.
Manx: Ta Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh er ghoaill orroo hene dy chooilleeney cur er y Chorpus ooilley ny recortyssyn jeh loayrtee dooghyssagh ta ry-gheddyn, as mac-screeunyn daa-hengagh jeu maroo.

English: To ensure comprehensive coverage, they will continue expanding their database to include more nineteenth and twentieth-century materials, reflecting the evolution of the language over time as well as aspects of the island’s social history.
Manx: Dy yannoo shickyr dy vel yn Corpus jeh roshtyn vooar, nee ad tannaghtyn dy vooadaghey nyn stoyr-fysseree dy ghoaill stiagh ny smoo stooghyn veih’n nuyoo cheead jeig as yn ‘eedoo cheead, ta soilshaghey kys ta’n ghlare er chaghlaa trooid traa chammah’s cooishyn ass shennaghys sheshoil yn Ellan.

English: Beyond the immediate goals, they aim to explore innovative ways in which the Corpus can serve as the foundation for future resources, potentially leading to the development of new dictionaries and language tools.
Manx: Çheu-mooie jeh ny deanyn vees er nyn gooilleeney çhelleeragh, t’ad kiarail scrutaghey aghtyn noa-chliaghtagh ayndoo oddys yn Corpus ve myr undin son couyryn ‘sy traa ry-heet, oddagh leeideil gys croo fockleyryn noa as greienyn çhengey.

English: Mooinjer Veggey is actively preparing three series of books for publication, featuring works such as Fairytales from the Isle of Man by Dora Broome, translated by Brian Stowell, Aesop's Fables, translated by Edward Faragher (Ned Beg Hom Ruy), and Anansi Folktales, translated by Annie Kissack.
Manx: Ta Mooinjer Veggey dy breeoil jannoo aarloo three straneyn dy lioaryn ry-hoi cur magh, ny mast’ oc obbraghyn goll-rish Skeealyn-Ferrish ass Mannin liorish Dora Broome, çhyndaait liorish Brian Stowell, Skeeallyn Aesop, çhyndaait liorish Edward Faragher (Ned Beg Hom Ruy), as Skeeallyn Anansi, çhyndaait liorish Annie Kissack.

English: Culture Vannin will be publishing the second Diary of a Wimpy Kid translation, Roderick Aboo, translated by Adrian Cain.
Manx: Bee Culture Vannin cur magh yn nah hyndaays jeh Jeelane Tootagh, ta shen dy ghra Roderick Aboo, çhyndaait liorish Adrian Cain.

English: Yn Skimmee Gaelgagh is currently engaged in the development of a video-based grammar course, with a particular emphasis on involving young speakers.
Manx: Ta’n Skimmee Gaelgagh goaill ayns laue ec y traa t’ayn dy chroo coorse grammar feeshanagh, ta cur tastey er-lheh da goaill stiagh loayrtee aegey.

English: This initiative aims to make language learning more engaging and accessible.
Manx: Dean yn çhalee shoh t’eh dy yannoo gynsagh glare ny s’tayrnyssee as ny smoo foshlit.

English: In addition, the service will continue enhancing the Manx Corpus website, with plans to publish an additional 50 new documents on the site.
Manx: Chammah’s shoh, nee yn çhirveish tannaghtyn dy vooadaghey as jannoo ny share ynnyd-eggey yn Chorpus Gaelgagh, lesh kiarail dy chur 50 ny smoo dy heksyn noa er yn ynnyd.

English: This expansion will provide users with an even more extensive and valuable resource for exploring the Manx language and culture.
Manx: Bee yn mooadaghey shoh croo couyr eer ny smoo vees scanshoil erskyn towse da ymmydeyryn ta kiarail geddyn magh mychione y Ghaelg as cultoor Vannin.

English: Theme 2: Manx as an important part of national & cultural identity.
Manx: Cooish 2: Gaelg myr ayrn scanshoil jeh enney ashoonagh as cultooroil

English: Strategic Commitment
Manx: Dean Strateyshagh

English: • We will work together to ensure that Manx language continues to be visible, inclusive and accessible.
Manx: Nee mayd gobbragh cooidjagh dy yannoo shickyr dy jean yn Ghaelg tannaghtyn dy ve ry-akin mastey’n theay, foshlit da dy chooilley pheiagh as aashagh dy gheddyn.

English: • We will promote Manx as a valued part of our distinctive contemporary, cultural and national identity
Manx: Nee mayd cur y Ghaelg er e toshiaght myr ayrn scanshoil jeh’n enney ashoonagh as cultooroil er-lheh as noa-emshyragh ain.

English: • We will ensure that the advantages of bilingualism are recognised.
Manx: Nee mayd jannoo shickyr dy bee vondeishyn daa-hengaghys er nyn doiggal.

English: Progression
Manx: Goll er e hoshiaght

English: Mooinjer Veggey has been consistent in promoting the Manx language through their parent and tot groups, Possan Cloie, nursery programmes, and social media campaigns.
Manx: Ta Mooinjer Veggey er hannaghtyn dy chur yn Ghaelg er e toshiaght trooid nyn bossanyn paarant as lhiannoo, Possan Cloie, claareeyn roie-scoill as troddanyn meanyn sheshoil.

English: They have secured funding from the Manx Lottery Trust for their development officer to collaborate with other language communities on the Isle of Man, aiming to encourage Manx language usage and expand the network of active supporters.
Manx: T’ad er hickyraghey cooney argidoil veih Treisht y Chronghyr Manninagh son yn oaseir-lhiasee oc dy cho-obbraghey marish pobbleyn-glare elley ayns Mannin, dy ghreinnaghey ymmyd y Ghaelg as mooadaghey moggyl yn sleih ta pohlldal ee.

English: Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh has actively engaged in organising outreach events targeting both the local and international communities.
Manx: Ta Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh dy breeoil er ghoaill ayns laue dy reaghey taghyrtyn mastey’n theay ta jeerit er y phobble ynnydagh as er y cho-phobble eddyr-ashoonagh.

English: Notably, they hosted the ‘Language and Society in the Isle of Man’ conference in May 2023, which drew more than 100 participants, both in person and online.
Manx: Sheeu tastey y ghoaill dy jagh yn co-haggloo ‘Glare as Sheshaght ayns Mannin’ er cummal ayns Mannin fo stiurey Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh ayns Mee ny Boaldyn 2023, as hayrn shen ny shlee na 100 dy ‘leih dy ghoaill ayrn, chammah dy persoonagh as er-linney.

English: Additionally, a lecture and a seminar on the future of Manx research formed part of the programme for the recent Cooish Festival.
Manx: Ny sodjey, va leaght as seminar mychione yn traa ry-heet jeh ronsaghey Gaelgagh er claare feailley yn Chooish hie er cummal er y gherrid.

English: In July 2023, the Culture Vannin Manx Gaelic Summer School made its debut, coinciding with Yn Chruinnaght Celtic Gathering.
Manx: Ayns Mee S’jerree yn Touree 2023, hie yn Scoill Souree Ghaelgagh er cummal son y chied cheayrt, ec y traa cheddin va’n Chruinnaght Celtic Gathering goll er.

English: The school attracted students not only from the Isle of Man but also from North America, garnering positive feedback.
Manx: Ren yn scoill souree tayrn stiagh studeyryn cha nee ynrican veih Mannin agh veih America Twoaie neesht, as va feedback feer vie ayn.

English: This will now be an annual event held at the end of July.
Manx: Hed shoh er cummal dy reiltagh maghey shoh ec jerrey Mee S’jerree yn Touree.

English: During the Cooish Festival 2022, a total of fourteen events catering for all ages and skill levels, including Island-wide pop-up lessons, were held.
Manx: Choud’s va’n feailley Cooish goll er ayns 2022, va kiare taghyrtyn jeig er nyn gummal, kiarail cour dy chooilley eash as leval dy schlei, goaill stiagh lessoonyn pop-up fud yn Ellan.

English: The festival was coordinated by Culture Vannin’s Greinneyder, with support from Jeebin and the wider community.
Manx: Hie yn feailley er stiurey liorish yn Ghreinneyder voish Culture Vannin, lesh cooney veih Jeebin as yn pobble cadjin.

English: The Londeyr award, recognising everyday use of Manx, was presented to Douglas East MHK Joney Faragher and Fynoderee Distillery founder Tiffany Kerruish for their work to shine a light on the language.
Manx: Va’n aundyr Londeyr, ta cur enney er ymmyd laaoil jeh’n Ghaelg, er ny choyrt da OKF son Doolish Hiar Joney Faraghey as da Tiffany Kerruish hug er bun thie-imbyl Fynoderee son nyn obbyr oc dy haishbyney yn ghlare.

English: Manx language provision has been incorporated into the Department of Education, Sport and Culture’s (DESC) Introduction to the Isle of Man course for all new teachers and Tynwald Members have also received specialist training sessions focusing on Manx language awareness and parliamentary Manx.
Manx: Ta kiarail cour gynsagh Gaelg er ve currit stiagh ayns y choorse ta cur er enney Mannin liorish yn Rheynn Ynsee, Spoyrt as Cultoor, ta ry-gheddyn son dy chooilley ynseyder noa as ta olteynyn Tynwald er gheddyn seshoonyn traenal er-lheh mychione tushtey jeh’n Ghaelg as jannnoo ymmyd jee ayns yn ardwhaiyl.

English: Yn Skimmee Gaelgagh has provided Manx lessons in schools upon request and actively promoted the use of Manx within educational institutions.
Manx: Ta’n Skimmee Gaelgagh er chiarail lessoonyn Gaelgagh ayns scoillyn tra v’eh shirrit as t’ad dy breeoil er chur er e hoshiaght ymmyd y Ghaelg çheu-sthie jeh sheshaghtyn-ynsee.

English: They have introduced introductory classes about the language to all new teachers on the island and have compiled a list of recommended measures endorsed by international bodies such as the Council of Europe, UNESCO, and OSACE.
Manx: T’ad er chur er bun brastyllyn-toshee mychione y ghlare cour dy chooilley ynseyder noa er yn ellan as t’ad er chur ry-cheilley rolley dy haaseyn moyllit as pohlldit liorish sheshaghtyn eddyr-ashoonagh goll-rish Coonceil yn Oarpey, UNESCO as OSACE.

English: A Certificate for Celtic Language Teaching, delivered by a retired CELTA instructor, is being offered to enhance the teaching skills of educators and explore contemporary theories of language acquisition.
Manx: Ta Teisht Ynsagh Çhengaghyn Celtiagh, er ny ynsagh liorish ynseyder CELTA ny haaue goll er çhebbal dy vishaghey schleiyn ynsee ynseyderyn as dy scrutaghey sheinyssyn noa-emshyragh mychione cosney stiagh glare.

English: Efforts have also been made to raise the profile of the Manx language in various media outlets, including Isle of Man Newspapers and Manx Radio, as well as on social media.
Manx: Ta eabyn er ve jeant dy hroggal enney er y Ghaelg mastey caghlaaghyn ammyr meanyn, goaill stiagh Pabyryn Naight Ellan Vannin as Radio Vannin, chammah’s er meanyn sheshoil.

English: Future Work
Manx: Obbyr ‘sy Traa Ry-Heet

English: Yn Skimmee Gaelgagh has plans to extend Manx language lessons to staff members at QE2 High School.
Manx: Ta fo’n Skimmee Gaelgagh dy heeyney magh lessoonyn Gaelgagh da olteynyn jeh skimmee Ardscoill BE2.

English: Additionally, they are committed to organising ongoing outreach events and other initiatives to engage with the broader community.
Manx: Chammah’s shoh t’ad er ghoaill orroo hene dy reaghey taghyrtyn mastey’n theay as shaleeyn elley dy hayrn stiagh yn pobble cadjin.

English: Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh aims to promote academic research on Manx-related subjects, including linguistics, literature, language revitalisation and language policy and planning.
Manx: Ta Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh kiarail dy chur er e hoshiaght ronsaghey ard-schoillaragh er cooishyn Gaelgagh, ny mast’oc ta glareydys, lettyraght, aavioghey glare, polasee glare as plannal glare.

English: They will also provide guidance and support to researchers interested in exploring Manx language topics.
Manx: Ver ad leeideilys as cooney da ronseyderyn ta sym oc er feddyn magh ny smoo mychione cooishyn Gaelgagh.

English: Mooinjer Veggey has ambitious projects in the pipeline, including the creation of a monthly podcast that will delve into the Manx language and explore other languages used in the Isle of Man.
Manx: Ta shaleeyn gloyrviandagh er ny birranyn ec Mooinjer Veggey, goaill stiagh croo podchreeley meeoil vees scrutaghey yn Ghaelg as glaraghyn elley ta ymmyd jeant jeu ayns Mannin.

English: Additionally, they plan to produce short bilingual films in Manx, potentially incorporating other minority languages and providing English subtitles for wider accessibility.
Manx: Chammah’s shoh, t’ad kiarail cur magh filmyn giare daa-hengagh ayns Gaelg, oddagh goaill stiagh myn-ghlaraghyn elley lesh fo-hitleyn Baarlagh dy roshtyn ny smoo dy ‘leih.

English: Theme 3: Manx for the Manx speaking community
Manx: Cooish 3: Gaelg son y phobble Gaelgagh

English: Strategic Commitment
Manx: Dean strateyshagh

English: • We will support the social use of the Manx language at home, work, in schools and educational settings and in free time.
Manx: Nee mayd ymmyd sheshoil ny Gaelgey y phohlldal ec y thie, ec yn obbyr, ayns scoillyn as ayns traa seyr.

English: • We will work to ensure the language is inclusive and reflects contemporary society.
Manx: Nee mayd gobbragh dy hickyraghey dy vel yn çhengey foshlit da dy chooilley pheiagh as dy vel ee cooie da sheshaght ny laghyn t’ayn nish.

English: • We will support parents who raise their children as Manx language speakers, and those who send their children to Manx medium education, and develop opportunities for social use of the Manx language.
Manx: Nee mayd cooney lesh paarantyn ta troggal nyn gloan myr loayrtee Gaelgagh, as lhieusyn ta cur nyn gloan dy ve ynsit trooid Gaelg, as nee mayd caaghyn y chroo son ymmyd sheshoil y Ghaelg.

English: • We will promote good communication between language groups both nationally and internationally.
Manx: Nee mayd cur er e hoshiaght co-haggloo eddyr possanyn glare chammah ayns Mannin as ‘sy theihll eddyr-ashoonagh.

English: Progression
Manx: Goll er e hoshiaght

English: Bunscoill Ghaelgagh has provided significant opportunities for communication and community use of Manx during the year.
Manx: Ta’n Vunscoill Ghaelgagh er chiarail caaghyn scanshoil son co-haggloo as son jannoo ymmyd jeh’n Ghaelg mastey’n phobble myleeaney.

English: These include events such as the Bunscoill Ghaelgagh Biosphere Bee picnic, a spectacular outdoor ‘son et lumière’ Christmas performance, and writing and staging a Manx musical version of The Jungle Book, Lioar y Doofyr.
Manx: Taghyrtyn lheid as Picnic Shellan Biosphere y Vunscoill Ghaelgagh, cuirrey-kiaull çheu-mooie yindyssagh ‘son et lumière’ ec y Nollick, as screeu as cur lesh dys yn ardan kiaull-chloie jeh Lioar y Doofyr.

English: These events have also been made available online for wider accessibility.
Manx: Ta ny taghyrtyn shoh ry-gheddyn er-linney dy yannoo ad foshlit da ny smoo dy ‘leih.

English: In conjunction with Mooinjer Veggey, the Bunscoill Ghaelgagh provided the Aundyr Verree Dhone challenge, with the 2023 Manx geocache challenge being won by a parent of past pupils.
Manx: As ad co-obbraghey marish Mooinjer Veggey, ren y Vunscoill Ghaelgagh doolane Aundyr Verree Dhone y chiarail, as va doolane geocache Vannin 2023 er ny chosney liorish paarant jeh ynseydagh dy row.

English: In addition, the Bunscoill Ghaelgagh has actively collaborated with other language communities, hosting a school from Ireland and welcoming Welsh visitors and their tutor.
Manx: Ny sodjey, ta’n Vunscoill Ghaelgagh dy breeoil er cho-obbraghey marish pobbleyn glare elley, goltaghey paitçhyn scoill voish Nerin as keayrtee as nyn ynseyder voish y Thalloo Vretnagh.

English: The school engaged in a cultural exchange by visiting Ennis in Ireland, with Manx-speaking parents accompanying the children.
Manx: Ghow yn scoill ayrn ayns coonrey cultooroil liorish cur shilley er Inish (Ennis) ayns Nerin, as paarantyn Gaelgagh goll marish ny paitçhyn.

English: Efforts to promote the use of Manx are ongoing through social media channels.
Manx: Ta eabyn dy chur er e hoshiaght ymmyd y Ghaelg goll er trooid ny meanyn sheshoil.

English: The school makes a weekly visit to Manx learners in a local café and provides classes for parent learners.
Manx: Ta’n scoill cur shilley er sleih ta gynsagh Gaelg gagh shiaghtin ayns thie-caffee ynnydagh as ta’n scoill kiarail brastyllyn cour paarantyn ta gynsagh yn ghlare.

English: Bunscoill teacher, Aalin Clague, has written and arranged a collection of accessible songs for Un Choraa Manx Gaelic community choir, comprising parents, friends, and children.
Manx: Ta Aalin Clague, ben-ynsee ec y Vunscoill Ghaelgagh, er screeu as er reaghey caghlaaghyn arrane aashagh da cor y phobble Un Choraa, ta goaill stiagh paarantyn, caarjyn as cloan.

English: The choir made their debut at the Guild (Manx Music Festival) in April and have performed at various events and venues.
Manx: Ghow yn cor arrane dy theayagh son y chied cheayrt ec y Ghuild (Feailley Kiaull Vannin) ayns Mee S’jerree y Niarree as t’ad er ghoaill arrane ec caghlaaghyn taghyrt as boayl.

English: Opportunities have been created for adult language learners to volunteer at the Bunscoill Ghaelgagh, strengthening intergenerational links and fostering a sense of community.
Manx: Ta caaghyn er ve er nyn groo da sleih aasit ta gynsagh Gaelg dy chooney er aght arryltagh ec y Vunscoill Ghaelgagh, ta niartaghey kianglaghyn eddyr sheelogheyn as ta bishaghey ennaghtyn pobble.

English: Several media outlets, including France 24, an Italian TV company, an Irish language radio station (Radió Fáilte), and a Tyrolean television company, have shown interest in the Bunscoill Ghaelgagh and the wider language community in the context of global language revitalisation .
Manx: Ta shiartanse dy ammyryn meanyn, ny mast’ oc France 24, co-lught çhellveeish Iddaalagh, stashoon radio Yernish (Raidió Fáilte), as co-lught çhellveeish Tyroleanagh er chur scansh er y Vunscoill Ghaelgagh as y phobble glare cadjin myr ayrn jeh cooish aavioghey glareyn feiy ny cruinney.

English: These media interactions have contributed to raising awareness not only of Manx but also the Isle of Man internationally.
Manx: Ta ny hammyryn meanyn shoh er chur cooney dy hroggal toiggal as fys cha nee ny lomarcan er y Ghaelg, agh er Mannin neesht er aght eddyr-ashoonagh.

English: Members of Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh have a diverse range of expertise and are actively engaged in research and advocacy across various minority languages.
Manx: Ta caghlaaghyn keint dy cheeayll chionnit as schlei ec olteynyn Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh as t’ad dy breeoil goaill ayns laue dy ronsaghey as loayrt magh son y Ghaelg ayns commeeys rish caghlaaghyn myn-ghlare.

English: This unique position allows them to establish valuable connections between the Manx community and other minority language communities, particularly in the Celtic countries.
Manx: Ta’n ynnyd er-lheh shoh cur kied daue dy chur er bun co-chianglaghyn ymmydoil eddyr yn pobble Gaelgagh as pobbleyn myn-ghlaraghyn elley, erskyn ooilley ayns ny çheeraghyn Celtiagh.

English: The ‘Language and Society in the Isle of Man’ conference showcased this connection, with attendees representing languages such as Irish, Welsh, and Guernsey French, among others.
Manx: Ren yn co-haggloo ‘Glare as Sheshaght ayns Mannin’ taishbyney yn co-chiangley shoh. Haink sleih va shassoo son glaraghyn lheid as Yernish, Bretnish, as Frangish Ghuernsee, mastey feallee elley.

English: The Manx Corpus website continues to expand and has become a popular tool for both learners and fluent speakers to research their language usage.
Manx: Ta ynnyd-eggey yn Chorpus gaase as bishaghey as t’eh er jeet dy ve ny ghreie ymmydoil cour sleih ta gynsagh chammah’s loayrtee flaaoil dy ronsaghey kys t’ad jannoo ymmyd jeh nyn nglare.

English: The Corpus and other resources that Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh aims to develop are intended to benefit all sectors of the Manx-speaking community, contributing to language preservation and revitalisation efforts.
Manx: Ta’n Corpus as couyryn elley ta Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh goaill ayns laue dy lhiasaghey kiarit da foays dy chooilley ayrn jeh’n phobble Gaelgagh, vees cooney lesh eabyn dy ‘reayll as aavioghey yn ghlare.

English: The Gaelic Broadcasting Committee has become a full sub-committee of Culture Vannin, with Yn Greinneyder as a member.
Manx: Ta Bing Ymskeaylley Gaelgagh er jeet dy ve ny fo-ving slane jeh Culture Vannin, as Yn Greinneyder ny oltey.

English: This committee has been actively working to identify the key needs of the Manx language community in consultation with the public.
Manx: Ta’n ving shoh er ve gobbragh dy gheddyn magh ard-femeyn y phobble Gaelgagh as ad co-loayrt rish y theay.

English: The team at Yn Skimmee Gaelgagh regularly uses the Manx language with pupils at schools, both inside and outside of formal lessons.
Manx: Ta’n Skimmee Gaelgagh jannoo ymmyd jeh’n Ghaelg dy reiltagh marish paitçhyn ayns ny scoillyn, chammah çheu-sthie as çheu-mooie jeh lessoonyn formoil.

English: They provide lessons to all pupils interested in learning Manx, including fluent speakers who have opted out of bilingual provision at QE2 High School.
Manx: T’ad çhebbal lessoonyn da dy chooilley ynseydagh ta cur scansh er gynsagh Gaelg, goaill stiagh loayrtee flaaoil ta er reih gyn kiarail daa-hengagh y gheddyn ec Ardscoill BE2.

English: Opportunities have been provided for teachers to spend time at Bunscoill Ghaelgagh, further promoting language learning and exchange.
Manx: Ta caaghyn er ve crooit son fir as mraane-ynsee dy cheau traa ec y Vunscoill Ghaelgagh, ta cur er nyn doshiaght gynsagh glare as coonrey glare eer ny sodjey.

English: Members of the Skimmee have joined global initiatives such as the UNESCO International Decade for Indigenous Languages Forum and the Global Language Rights Network, contributing to the broader discussion on language preservation and revitalisation.
Manx: Ta olteynyn y Skimmee Gaelgagh er ghoaill ayrn ayns shaleeyn feiy ny cruinney lheid as Forym Jeih Bleeaney Eddyr-ashoonagh UNESCO son Glaraghyn Dooghyssagh as Moggyl Cairyssyn Glaragh Feiy ny Cruinney, cur ny smooinaghtyn ocsyn gys yn cho-loayrtys s’feayn mychione freayll as aavioghey glaraghyn.

English: The Skimmee’s Manx Language Officer is actively involved in the British Irish Council Officers Group for Indigenous, Minority, and Lesser-Used Languages, as well as reporting to the Council of Europe Committee of Experts of the European Charter for Regional or Minority Languages, elevating the profile of Manx language efforts on international platforms.
Manx: Ta Oaseir yn Skimmee Gaelgagh goaill ayrn breeoil ayns Possan Oaseiryn Coonceil Ghoaldagh-Yernagh son Glaraghyn Dooghyssagh, Myn-Ghlaraghyn, as Glaraghyn ta Ymmyd Sloo jeant jeu, chammah’s cur coontey da Bing Coonceil yn Oarpey dy Oaylleeyn Conaant Coonceil yn Oarpey son Glaraghyn Ardjynagh ny Myn-Ghlaraghyn, ta troggal enney mastey pobbleyn eddyr-ashoonagh er shaleeyn Gaelgagh.

English: In May, the team contributed to the ‘Language and Society in the Isle of Man’ conference hosted by the University of Galway and Manx National Heritage.
Manx: Ayns Mee ny Boaldyn, ren yn Skimmee cur cooney da’n cho-haggloo ‘Glare as Sheshaght ayns Mannin’ hie er cummal liorish Ollooscoill Ghalliu as Eiraght Ashoonagh Vannin.

English: Additionally, in August, the Manx Language Officer chaired two sessions at the International Congress of Celtic Studies at Utrecht University, further promoting the Manx language on the international stage.
Manx: Ny sodjey, ayns y Chied Vee jeh’n Ouyr, va’n Oaseir Gaelgagh ayns y stoyl-drommey son daa heshoon ec Co-hionnal Eddyr-ashoonagh Studeyryssyn Celtiagh ec Ollooscoill Utrecht, cur yn Ghaelg er e toshiaght ny sodjey mastey ashoonyn y theihll.

English: Future Work
Manx: Obbyr ‘sy Traa Ry-Heet

English: Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh aims to encourage and include all sectors of the community in their work and provide opportunities for skills development among groups underrepresented in the Manx language community, especially younger people.
Manx: Ta dean ec Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh dy ghreinnaghey as goaill stiagh dy chooilley cherroo jeh’n phobble ayns nyn obbyr as cur caaghyn dy lhiasaghey schleiyn mastey possanyn gyn çhaghterys dy liooar ayns y phobble Gaelgagh, erskyn ooilley sleih aegey.

English: Mooinjer Veggey has successfully secured funding to promote and expand Possan Cloie Parent and Tot groups across the Isle of Man, with plans to establish another group in the coming year.
Manx: T’eh er jeet lesh Mooinjer Veggey dy gheddyn cooney argidoil dy chur er nyn doshiaght as mooadaghey possanyn paarant as lhiannoo ‘Possan Cloie’ fud Mannin, as plannyn oc dy chur er bun possan elley ayns y vlein shoh çheet.

English: These groups play a vital role in nurturing language development among the younger population and providing families with an interest in bilingual education, ensuring a healthy intake for Bunscoill Ghaelgagh in subsequent years.
Manx: Ta ny possanyn shoh cloie ayrn ymmyrçhagh ayns bishaghey’n ghlare mastey sleih aegey as greinnaghey lughtyn-thie dy chur scansh er ynsagh daa-hengagh, vees shickyraghey dy bee paitçhyn dy liooar ayn dy gholl stiagh ‘sy Vunscoill Ghaelgagh ayns ny bleeantyn ry-heet.

English: The community engagement project Gaelg my Vlaa has a clear mission to involve all segments of the community, aiming to increase the number of people using and supporting the Manx language.
Manx: Ta dean baghtal ec yn çhalee ‘Gaelg my Vlaa’, ta shen dy ghra tayrn stiagh dy chooilley cherroo jeh’n phobble, as shirrey dy vishaghey yn earroo dy ‘leih ta jannoo ymmyd jeh’n Ghaelg as ta pohlldal yn ghlare.

English: The Gaelic Broadcasting Committee is working on developing a comprehensive strategy for Manx Gaelic broadcasting in the latter part of 2023, potentially expanding the reach of the language through media.
Manx: Ta Bing Ymskeaylley Gaelgagh gobbragh er lhiasaghey strateysh jeh roshtyn vooar son ymskeaylley Gaelgagh ayns yn ayrn s’jerree jeh 2023, oddagh mooadaghey roshtyn yn ghlare trooid ny meanyn.

English: There is a possibility of hosting the International Congress of Celtic Studies in 2031 in the Isle of Man, with initial discussions and cost assessments already underway, offering an exciting opportunity to showcase Manx culture and language on an international platform.
Manx: Oddagh Co-hionnal Eddyr-Ashoonagh Studeyryssyn Celtiagh goll er cummal ayns Mannin ayns 2031, as ta resoonaghtyn toshee as sess costyn fo-raad hannah. Ta shoh cur caa greesagh dy haishbyney cultoor as glare Vannin mastey ashoonyn y theihll.

English: There are plans to actively involve young speakers who have received education in Manx Medium Education in book publishing and video projects, ensuring their active participation in language preservation and revitalisation efforts.
Manx: Ta plannyn ayn dy ghoaill stiagh er aght breeoil loayrtee aegey, hooar nyn ynsagh trooid y Ghaelg, ayns cur magh lioaryn as shaleeyn feeshan, vees shickyraghey dy gow ad ayrn breeoil ayns çhebbyn dy ‘reayll as aavioghey yn ghlare.

English: Looking further ahead, Jeebin is starting planning towards a coordinated celebration of the Manx Language in 2026.
Manx: Jeeaghyn rhymboo ny sodjey, ta Jeebin goaill toshiaght dy yannoo plannyn ry-hoi ardeailley co-oardit jeh’n Ghaelg ayns 2026.

English: Theme 4: Lifelong learning of the Manx language from early to later years
Manx: Cooish 4: Ynsagh feiy’n vea voish oikanys dys shenn-eash

English: Strategic Commitment
Manx: Dean Strateyshagh

English: • We will ensure that anyone, of any age, anywhere, has the opportunity to learn and speak the Manx language and that the language’s role in community cohesion is recognised.
Manx: Nee mayd shickyraghey dy bee ec peiagh erbee, boayl erbee, caa dy ynsagh as dy loayrt y Ghaelg as dy bee scansh y ghlare son cooidjaghtys y phobble er ny toiggal.

English: • We will support the development of a clear pathway and training programme for the recruitment and retention of language practitioners in educational settings.
Manx: Nee mayd cooney lesh croo cassan ynsee as claare traenal shickyr son geddyn as freayll obbreeyn Gaelgagh ayns buill ynsee.

English: • We will work to increase the numbers of speakers and learners of Manx to 5000 by 2032.
Manx: Nee mayd gobbragh dy vishaghey yn earroo dy loayrtee as ynseydee yn Ghaelg dys 5,000 roish 2032.

English: • We will explore ways to grow provision for the language.
Manx: Nee mayd shirrey magh aghtyn dy vishaghey mooadys y chooney ta kiarit da’n ghlare.

English: Progression
Manx: Goll er e hoshiaght

English: Manx National Heritage supports the learning of Manx, providing spaces for Manx classes and Manx language community groups.
Manx: Ta Eiraght Ashoonagh Vannin pohlldal gynsagh Gaelg, as kiarail buill son brastyllyn Gaelgagh as possanyn pobble Gaelgagh.

English: Mooinjer Veggey has achieved success in securing funding from the Manx Lottery Trust and continues to explore other funding avenues, ensuring the organisation's ability to sustain support for pre-school, primary, and parental learning initiatives.
Manx: T’eh er jeet lesh Mooinjer Veggey dy gheddyn cooney argidoil veih Treisht y Chronghyr Manninagh as t’ad tannaghtyn dy hirrey magh saaseyn elley dy gheddyn cooney argidoil, vees shickyraghey dy vod yn çheshaght tannaghtyn dy chur cooney da cooishyn roie-scoill, bunscoill as cour sleih aasit.

English: In May, Bunscoill Ghaelgagh hosted a seminar from school principal and Irish language activist Dónal Ó hAiniféin to discuss immersion language learning.
Manx: Ayns Mee ny Boaldyn, hie seminar er cummal ec y Vunscoill Ghaelgagh, she ard-ynseyder as fer breeoil ayns cooishyn Yernish Dónal Ó hAiniféin va’n loayrtagh ren loayrt mychione gynsagh glare liorish thummey.

English: This resulted in developing more collaboration between Yn Skimmee Gaelgagh and Bunscoill Ghaelgagh as they explore the concept of a Manx Gaelic ecosystem, offering continuous professional development opportunities while fostering knowledge exchange and idea sharing.
Manx: Yn eiyrtys jeh shoh, v’eh dy row ny smoo co-obbraghey currit er bun eddyr Yn Skimmee Gaelgagh as yn Vunscoill Ghaelgagh choud’s t’ad ronsaghey yn smooinaght jeh ecosystem Gaelgagh, vees çhebbal caaghyn son lhiasaghey keirdey agh ec y traa cheddin greinnaghey coonrey fysseree as rheynn smooinaghtyn.

English: Increased interaction and outreach between Bunscoill Ghaelgagh and the Mooinjer Veggey pre-school group at the nearby school has led to a new intake of 14 pupils, representing a 100% increase from last year.
Manx: Eiyrtys tooilley eddyr-obbraghey as roshtyn magh yn derrey yeh dys y jeh elley eddyr yn Vunscoill Ghaelgagh as possan roie-scoill Vooinjer Veggey ta ry-gheddyn ayns scoill er-gerrey, shen dy vel 14 paitçhyn noa çheet stiagh, ta cowraghey bishaghey jeh 100% neayr’s nurree.

English: Sheshaght ny Paarantyn, the parent's group responsible for organising events for children and families at the school, plays a pivotal role in welcoming the new intake and encouraging their participation in life at the school.
Manx: Ta Sheshaght ny Paarantyn, yn possan ta goaill ayns laue yn currym dy reaghey taghyrtyn son cloan as lughtyn-thie t’ec y scoill, goaill ayrn scanshoil ayns goltaghey ny paitçhyn noa as greinnaghey ad dy ghoaill ayrn ayns bea yn scoill.

English: Culture Vannin continues to offer weekly Manx lessons for adult learners, including three morning classes that coincide with school drop-off times, and three Zoom classes to widen accessibility to Manx learning.
Manx: Ta Culture Vannin tannaghtyn dy hebbal lessoonyn Gaelgagh gagh shiaghtin son sleih aasit ta gynsagh, goaill stiagh three lessoonyn moghree ta goaill ynnyd ec y traa cheddin as ta paarantyn cur lhieu nyn gloan dy scoill, as three brastyllyn Zoom dy ‘osley gynsagh Gaelg da ny smoo dy ‘leih.

English: As of September 2023, there are 9 weekly adult classes which are progressive, and follow the Loayr Gaelg course.
Manx: Voish Mee Veanagh yn Ouyr 2023, ta 9 brastyllyn keimnagh son sleih aasit er ve goll er gagh shiaghtin ta geiyrt er y choorse Loayr Gaelg.

English: Culture Vannin has also run well- attended in-person and online Manx language taster courses, as well as language and song workshops, intensive weekend Manx courses and talks throughout the year.
Manx: Ta Culture Vannin er stiurey coorseyn blass ny Gaelgey er-linney as dy persoonagh va dy mie taaghit neesht, chammah’s keirdlannyn glare as arrane, coorseyn Gaelgagh jeean ec y jerrey shiaghtin as loayrtyssyn car ny bleeaney.

English: The development of the Department of Education, Sport and Culture Manx Language Charter has reached a draft stage, offering a clear framework that all schools can use to promote and increase the use of Manx Gaelic by children within a whole school context.
Manx: Ta lhiasaghey Conaant Gaelgagh y Rheynn Ynsee, Spoyrt as Cultoor er roshtyn keim dreaght, as t’eh çhebbal crauelagh vaghtal oddys dy chooilley scoill ymmyd y yannoo jee dy chur er e hoshiaght as mooadaghey ymmyd y Ghaelg liorish paitçhyn ayns seihll y clane scoill.

English: An action plan for implementation is also in the draft stages.
Manx: Ta plan-jantys cour cur ayns bree yn conaant ayns cummey dreaght neesht.

English: Efforts are being made to ensure that all pupils in Island schools who wish to learn Manx have the opportunity to do so.
Manx: Ta eabyn goll er jannoo dy yannoo shickyr dy vel caa ec dy chooilley ynseydagh ayns scoillyn yn Ellan ta geearree gynsagh Gaelg, shen y yannoo.

English: For fluent speaking pupils at QE2, lessons are provided in Key Stage 3, with an additional extra-curricular session per week in Key Stage 4.
Manx: Cour ynseydee flaaoil ec BE2, ta lessoonyn kiarit ayns Ard-Cheim 3, lesh un seshoon elley çheu-mooie jeh’n churriglym gagh shiaghtin ayns Ard-Cheim 4.

English: Similar lessons, sometimes extracurricular, are offered to fluent-speaking pupils in Key Stages 3 and 4 upon request.
Manx: Ta lessoonyn casley roo shoh, ny cheayrtyn çheu-mooie jeh’n churriglym, goll er çhebbal da ynseydee ta loayrt dy flaaoil ayns Ard-Cheimyn 3 as 4 tra t’ad er nyn shirrey.

English: Future Work
Manx: Obbyr ‘sy Traa Ry-Heet

English: As resources permit, Mooinjer Veggey has ambitious plans to establish three additional Possan Cloie groups, potentially introducing seven hundred and fifty new Manx supporters and speakers to complement the five hundred individuals they are already fostering through their existing groups.
Manx: Croghey er couyryn dy liooar ve ry-gheddyn, ta plannyn gloyrviandagh ec Mooinjer Veggey dy chur er bun three Possanyn Cloie elley, oddagh, t’eh laik, cur lesh stiagh shiaght cheead as jeih as daeed dy ‘leih noa ta pohlldal as loayrt yn Ghaelg, vees currit da’n wheig cheead dy ‘leih t’ad kiarail er nyn son hannah lesh ny possanyn t’oc nish.

English: In essence, this effort represents approximately half of the new speakers needed to achieve the ambitious goal of reaching 5,000 speakers by 2032.
Manx: Ayns focklyn giarey, ta’n eab shoh cowraghey mysh lieh jeh ny loayrtee noa ta ymmyrçhagh dy chooilleeney yn dean gloyrviandagh oc, ta shen dy ghra roshtyn 5000 loayrtagh roish 2032.

English: To ensure a sustainable future for Mooinjer Veggey over the next decade, they are diligently working on a new fundraising strategy aimed at enhancing stability and support for the organisation.
Manx: Dy hickyraghey traa ry-heet fondagh son Mooinjer Veggey harrish ny jeih bleeaney ta roin, t’ad gobbragh dy jeidjagh er strateysh noa son troggal argid er-çhee mooadaghey shickyrys as cooney son yn çheshaght.

English: The Manx Language Charter sub-group is actively collaborating with the DESC to share their plans and seek approval and support for piloting the charter within a pilot group of local schools.
Manx: Ta fo-phossan yn Chonaant Gaelgagh co-obbraghey dy breeoil marish y RYSP dy rheynn nyn blannyn as shirrey aigney mie as cooney son prowal yn conaant çheu-sthie jeh possan-toshee dy scoillyn ynnydagh.

English: Yn Skimmee Gaelgagh hopes to maintain the current levels of provision on offer moving forward and is exploring various alternatives to bring young speakers in Key Stages 4 and 5 together for Manx Medium activities, fostering their language development and engagement.
Manx: Ta’n Skimmee Gaelgagh treishteil dy chummal seose levalyn dy chiarail ta ry-gheddyn ec y traa t’ayn as goll er nyn doshiaght, chammah’s shoh t’ad ronsaghey caghlaaghyn saase elley dy chur lesh loayrtee aegey ayns Ard-Cheim 4 as 5 ry-cheilley son roortyssyn Gaelgagh, vees bishaghey nyn schleiyn lesh y ghlare as greinnaghey ad dy ghoaill taitnys ayns y ghlare.

English: Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh members are currently involved in research projects which will provide insights which should aid in the development of language policy in the future, including with regard to language practitioners in educational settings and achieving the target of 5000 learners and speakers.
Manx: Ta olteynyn Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh goaill ayns laue shaleeyn ronsee ec y traa t’ayn vees cur soilshey as fys vees cooney lesh lhiasaghey polasee glare ayns y traa ry-heet, goaill stiagh bentyn rish sleih ta jannoo ymmyd jeh’n ghlare ayns ynnydyn ynsee as cooilleeney yn dean jeh roshtyn 5000 ynseydagh as loayrtagh.

English: The Corpus and other resources they hope to develop are of use to those learning and teaching the language at all levels.
Manx: Ta’n Corpus as couyryn elley ta foue dy chroo ymmyrçhagh dauesyn ta gynsagh yn ghlare, as ta gynsagh yn ghlare da sleih elley, ec dy chooilley cheim.

English: They have also adopted an advocacy strategy outlining the developments they would like to see in the sector in the next decade.
Manx: T’ad er ghlackey strateysh cour shirrey foayr y theay neesht, ta soilshaghey shen ny b’laik lhieu fakin goll er lhiasaghey ayns y cherroo ayns ny jeih bleeaney ry-heet.

English: Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh will encourage other researchers to carry out research which has the potential to inform Manx language policy development.
Manx: Nee Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh ronseyderyn elley y ghreinnaghey dy yannoo ronsaghey oddagh ve ymmydoil son croo polasee Gaelgagh.

English: Conclusion
Manx: Jerrey

English: In summary, the collaborative efforts spearheaded by Jeebin in conjunction with the language community, as outlined in the second Manx Language Strategy, are aimed at ensuring the continued flourishing and maintenance of the Manx language for the next decade and beyond.
Manx: Slane bree y chooish shoh, t’eh dy vel ny heabyn dy cho-obbraghey t’er nyn imman liorish Jeebin cooidjagh marish pobble yn ghlare, myr ta soilshit magh ayns y nah Strateysh son y Ghaelg, t’ad soit er shickyraghey dy jean yn Ghaelg tannaghtyn dy vishaghey as dy bee meansal ayn er e son ayns ny jeih bleeaney ry-heet as ny sodjey.

English: The achievements in the initial year of implementing this strategic framework stand as a testament to the unwavering dedication and hard work exhibited by those immersed in the Manx language community.
Manx: Ta ny speeideilyssyn ayns y chied vlein va’n crauelagh strateyshagh shoh er ny coyrt ayns bree nyn shassoo myr feanish jeh jeidjys gyn leaystey as obbyr chreoi jeant ec yn sleih ta soit dy dowin ayns y phobble Gaelgagh.

English: Through Jeebin's ambitious vision and resolute commitment, we not only aim to sustain the progress achieved thus far but also aspire to be global leaders in the ongoing development and growth of the Manx language in the decades ahead.
Manx: Trooid ashlish ghloyrviandagh Jeebin, as nyn marrantys shassooagh, cha nel shin ny lomarcan kiarail dy chummal seose yn speeideilys t’ain choud shoh agh ta shin geearree ve nyn leeideilee dowanagh ayns lhiasaghey as aase yn Ghaelg ayns ny jeihyn dy vleeantyn ta roin.

English: The trajectory set by Jeebin, in partnership with the Manx language community and government, paints a promising picture for the flourishing future of the Manx language.
Manx: Ta’n cassan t’er ny hoiaghey liorish Jeebin, ayns boodeeys rish y phobble Gaelgagh as yn reiltys, croo co-chaslys gialdynagh jeh traa ry-heet ayn ta’n Ghlare Vanninagh bishaghey.
